commit.message:
fix: apply detect_zone as inclusion filter in counter eligibility

The detect_zone polygon was fetched from the DB but never applied.
False positives in the upper frame (y=0.44-0.50, outside the road
area) were reaching the counter and masking real vehicles.

Now:
- _detect_poly loaded from cameras.detect_zone on each refresh
- _eligible_mask() rejects vehicle centers outside the detect_zone
- Restored original count_line (correctly positioned inside detect_zone)
- Upgraded refresh log to INFO so zone config is always visible

commit.message:
Fix app back button double-pop: skip popstate when goBack() already handled

goBack() pops the view history stack then calls window.history.back() to
sync browser history. The popstate handler was re-popping the already-
shortened stack, causing navigation to jump to calendar instead of the
previous view. Now the popstate handler bails when handlingPopState flag
is already set by goBack().

Migrate to registerTool API and add detailed parameter descriptions

- Replace deprecated server.tool() with server.registerTool() across all 19 tools
- Add readOnlyHint annotation to all tools
- Add explicit valid values for protocol slugs and network identifiers in .describe()
- Helps AI models provide correct parameter values without guessing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(collector): merge getCraftingTasks() progress into getCraftingCPUs() job writes

getCraftingCPUs() covers all jobs (incl. player-terminal) but AP passes cpu=null
to parseCraftingJob so completion/crafted are always 0. getCraftingTasks() has
real progress but only for bridge-submitted jobs.

Now fetches both: builds a progress lookup from getCraftingTasks() keyed by item
name, then merges crafted/completion into each CPU job write. Terminal crafts
still appear but show 0% progress; bridge-submitted crafts show real progress.

Also adds cpu_index tag to ae_crafting_job writes for CPU correlation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix waveform not loading after recording

Use blocking set_param for file_path to ensure the DSP processes the
file load before querying file_info/waveform. The normal fire-and-forget
set_param in overtake mode was being overwritten by subsequent writes
to the single shared-memory IPC slot.

feat(client): replace Edit text buttons with Pencil icons in tables (MGG-277) (#105)

Replace "Edit" text in table action buttons with a Pencil icon from
lucide-react for a cleaner, more compact UI. Uses size="icon" and
aria-label="Edit" to maintain accessibility.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf(otel): defer OTLP exporter imports until first use (#1690)

Importing otel_utils was costing ~0.18s on every process start because
the gRPC and HTTP OTLP exporter packages (which pulls in requests) were
imported at module level. Since these exporters are only needed when a
traces endpoint is configured and listening, move their imports inside
get_otlp_exporter(). This halves xorq.vendor.ibis import time (0.58s →
0.25s) and speeds up all CLI commands that don't use OTLP tracing.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

kind-pasteur-2026-03-06-S23: endpoint parity analysis + 2-path-cover involution

Key finding: The global Even Cycle Vanishing involution CANNOT directly
apply to the endpoint setting because 2-path-covers have exactly 2 cycles
(both containing fixed vertices a,b). The cancellation of r^1 terms
at n=4 is a 6-way cancellation, not pairwise.

commit.message:
Fix overtake set_param race: make timeout variant always block

In overtake mode (overtake_mode >= 2), shadow_set_param is fire-and-forget
to avoid blocking UI on rapid encoder streams. But this causes critical
params like file_path to be lost when multiple rapid writes hit the single
shared-memory IPC slot.

shadow_set_param_timeout now bypasses fire-and-forget via force_blocking
parameter, and a new host_module_set_param_blocking JS shim exposes this
to overtake modules for critical operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
test: regression test for OCLEANA1 all-notify-fail graceful degradation (issue #7)

Real device (Oclean Air 1 / OCLEANA1) has no working notify characteristics:
fbb86/fbb90 lack a CCCD descriptor, fbb89 has no notify property, 6c290d2e is
absent. The integration already handles this gracefully (poll completes with
battery-only data, no crash). This commit protects that behaviour with tests.

- simulator.py: add with_notify_errors() builder method so start_notify raises
  BleakError for specified characteristic UUIDs
- test_simulator.py: add TestOcleanA1AllNotifyFail with three assertions:
  poll completes, battery==99, no session keys in result

   replacing, so historical data from custom range picker survives polls.

2. Intensity grid radius: extracted jma05ThresholdKm() to gmpe.ts as
   shared lookup (linear interpolation, M2.5-M8.0). Grid radius now
   scales continuously from 0.2deg (M2.5, 21km) to 10deg (M8.0, 846km).
   Previously M<5.0 was hardcoded at 2.0deg (222km) — a M3.2 showed
   intensity field 4x larger than physics allows.

3. Wave animation: MAX_RADIUS_KM replaced with magnitude-dependent
   maxRadiusForMag() using same GMPE threshold. M3.2 Fukuoka waves
   now stop at ~51km (not 800km reaching Korea).

commit.message:
fix: silent return for 0307 year_byte=0 on OCLEANY3P (issue #3)

OCLEANY3P sends a 0307 notification every poll with session_count=1 but
year_byte=0 (no inline session data). Previously this hit the
_device_datetime() ValueError path and logged a misleading
"Type-1 record parse error: implausible year 2000" every poll.

Added an early-return guard in _parse_info_t1_response() for payload[5]==0
that logs a clean debug message and returns {} instead.

Also adds regression tests for:
- OCLEANY3P zero-timestamp 0307 (TestRealOCLEANY3PZeroTimestamp0307)
- Two unknown notification types 021f/5100 emitted by OCLEANY3P
  (TestRealOCLEANY3PUnknownNotifications)

* feat: add fullscreen support for tool result cards

display configuration.

Fullscreen overlay:

R: passes `display$full_screen` through to <shiny-tool-result>

Also fixes pkgdown CI by adding brand.yml to workflow extra-packages.

commit.message:
Add Tucker decomposition pipeline for bilinear layer interpretability

Stages 1-2: Synthetic CP tensor verification and AND-gate task with
proper task labeling, shared-feature analysis, and ODM diagnostics.
Stage 4b: Three-layer multi-layer comparison (independent, bottom-up,
joint rotation strategies) with cross-layer overlap analysis.
Stage 5: BilinearGPT position-conditioned Tucker with RoPE, evidence
figures, and circuit-level Tucker factor views.

commit.message:
fix(bot): use DevExpress Clear button for LINEDISC workaround

The LINEDISC field is a xafLookupEditor where synthetic .click() on
the dropdown button doesn't open the popup (DevExpress needs real
mouse events). The previous approach fell back to typing N/A in the
visible input, which never changed the hidden _VI value that controls
the actual discount calculation.

Now uses the Clear button (B1) which has an inline onclick calling
ASPx.BEClick() — this properly resets the hidden value and triggers
server-side recalculation. Fallback calls ASPx.BEClick() directly.
